sections.paragraph:
of both genders, but more often f.-

(1) the tongue of men or of animals, Exo 11:7 and so frequently. (Arab. لسَانُ, Ethiop. ልሳን፡ Aram. לִשָּׁן, ܠܶܫܳܢ, also in languages not Phœnicio-Shemitic, Sanscrit rasana, Armen. լռեզւ liezu, Coptic ⲁⲁⲥ, and even γλῶσσα, in which γ is an addition; comp. λεύσσω, γλαύσσω, γνόφος, νέφος, χλαῖνα, lana and many other words. [Welsh, llais, voice, lleision, endued with voice.] The original idea is that of lapping, a power which is found in the syllable las, compare לָחַשׁ, لحس, لَسَّ לָשַׁר. A secondary root is the Poel לֹושֵׁן which see.) תַּחַת לְשֹׁון פּ׳ under the tongue of any one is used in Hebrew for in the mouth, Psa 10:7 66:17 . Specially used of a calumnious or malignant tongue (what the Chaldeans and Zabians call a third tongue, compare Sir_28:15 and the verb לָשַׁן). Psa 140:12, אִישׁ לָשֹׁון “a man of tongue,” i.e. a slanderer (but בַּעַל לָשֹׁון is an enchanter, Ecc 10:11). Jer 18:18, “come! let us smite him with the tongue,” i.e. as rightly given in the Chaldee, let us bear false witness against him. Job 5:21, שֹׁוט לָשֹׁון “the scourge of the tongue” (in German by a like figure, kflatfchen). Pro 10:31, לְשֹׁון תַּהְפֻּכֹות “a perverse tongue,” i.e. deceitful; 17:20 . Meton.-(a) for speech, Job 15:5, לִשֹׁון עֲרוּמִים “crafty speeches;” Pro 16:1.-(b) for idiom (German Sprache). Dan 1:4, לְשֹׁון כַּשְׂדִּים “the tongue of the Chaldeans.” Gen 10:5, אִישׁ לִלְשֹׁנֹו “every one according to his tongue,” and even-(c) a nation, a people, which used a peculiar language. Isa 66:18, כָּל־הַגֹּויִם וְהַלְּשֹׁנֹות “all nations and tongues” (see Chald. לִּשָּׁן ). It is applied-

(2) to inanimate things which resemble tongues-(a) לְשֹׁון זָהָב a tongue of gold, i.e. a bar of gold, Jos 7:21, 24 Jos 7:24. Vulg. regula aurea.-(b) לְשֹׁון אֵשׁ a flame of fire, so called from having some resemblance to a tongue, and seeming to lap like one, see לָהַב , לָהַט (Æn. ii. 684), compare γλῶσσαι ὡσεὶ πυρός, Act 2:3 Arabic لسان النار; Persic زبان آتش; zuboni atesh, Isa 5:24.-(c) לְשֹׁון הַיָּם Jos 15:5 18:19 Isa 11:15, and simply לָשֹׁון Jos 15:2, a bay of the sea (comp. Germ. Srdzunge); in the Arabian geographers لسان الجر.

[“לָשַׁךְ an unused root, perhaps if it be Phœnicio-Shemitic, i.q. שָׁלַךְ; but used intrans. to throw one-self down. Hence-”]
